description_long: This is a classic propeller-driven airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a rounded nose, featuring two engines mounted on the leading edges of its straight, low-mounted wings. The aircraft is painted primarily white with blue accents and has visible passenger windows along the side, suggesting it is designed for transport. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers in a conventional arrangement. The landing gear is visible and appears to be of the taildragger type, with two main wheels under the wings and a smaller wheel at the tail. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the pilots. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century passenger or cargo aircraft, suitable for both urban and regional airfields.
description: A white, twin-propeller airplane with blue accents, low-mounted straight wings, and a conventional tail, designed for passenger or cargo transport.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full fuselage, passenger windows, and both engines with propellers clearly visible.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tail section, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the full wingspan.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other side of the fuselage, engines, and landing gear.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the rounded nose, cockpit windows, and the twin propeller engines.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets; moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main wheels under the wings and a single tail wheel; fixed landing gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
